mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately suppress consumer spending expectations and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that sustains corporate earnings revisions and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during a post-shock recovery phase reflect accelerating capacity utilization and supply-side momentum that directly translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during post-shock recovery periods yield negative or flat 20-day forward returns in over 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product establishes a baseline trend of economic expansion that mechanically supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase above 0.4% forces monetary authorities to prioritize inflation control over growth, triggering immediate discount rate repricing that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above the 0.4% threshold consistently coincide with positive 60-day forward returns, breaking the negative correlation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market tightening that boosts wage growth expectations and corporate revenue forecasts, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal produces negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real personal consumption expenditures during a monetary tightening cycle signals immediate demand destruction that overrides liquidity support and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines during tightening cycles fail to predict negative 5-day forward returns, with positive returns occurring in over 65% of cases.
